feed.title: Current Watches, Warnings and Advisories for New Zealand issued by MetService
feed.rights: Copyright Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited, 2026. Licensed under Creative Commons BY 4.0.

entry.title: Heavy Rain Watch
entry.summary: Periods of heavy rain, amounts may approach warning criteria. Further rain is possible on Thursday. Impact: Streams and rivers may rise rapidly. Surface flooding, slips, and difficult driving conditions possible. Chance of upgrading to a Warning: High. Changes: Chance of upgrade increased to high.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Heavy Rain Warning - Orange
entry.summary: Expect 130 to 180 mm of rain. Peak rates of 15 to 25 mm/h during Tuesday. Impact: Streams and rivers may rise rapidly. Surface flooding, slips, and difficult driving conditions possible. Chance of upgrading to a Red Warning: Low.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Heavy Rain Warning - Red
entry.summary: Expect a further 250 to 350 mm and possibly up to 400 mm. Peak rates of 15 to 25 mm/h likely from tonight until Tuesday afternoon, especially about the ranges. During this period, coastal locations may receive 80 to 120 mm of rain. Freezing level about 1600 metres and snow likely above 1200 metres. Impact: Threat to life from dangerous river conditions, significant flooding and slips. Conditions will disrupt travel, make some roads impassable, and isolate communities.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Heavy Rain Warning - Orange
entry.summary: Expect a further 170 to 200 mm of rain. During the same period 60 to 100 mm is likely in areas near the coast. Peak rates of 15 to 25 mm/h expected tonight through to Tuesday afternoon, especially about the ranges. Freezing level about 1600 metres and snow likely above 1200 metres. Impact: Dangerous river conditions, flooding, and slips likely. Dangerous driving conditions and road closures are expected. Chance of upgrading to a Red Warning: High.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Heavy Rain Watch
entry.summary: Periods of heavy rain, and amounts may approach warning criteria. Chance of upgrading to a Warning: Moderate.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Heavy Rain Warning - Orange
entry.summary: Expect 120 to 150 mm of rain. Peak intensities 15 to 25 mm/h during Tuesday. Impact: Streams and rivers may rise rapidly. Surface flooding, slips, and difficult driving conditions possible. Chance of upgrading to a Red Warning: Low. Changes: Total forecast rainfall accumulation reduced slightly.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Heavy Rain Warning - Orange
entry.summary: Expect a further 100 to 140 mm of rain, especially about the foothills and high country region. A further 50 to 80 mm possible over the plains during the same period. Note this rain is expected to be falling as snow above 600 metres. Impact: Streams and rivers may rise rapidly. Surface flooding, slips, and difficult driving conditions possible. Chance of upgrading to a Red Warning: Moderate. Changes: Event extended a few hours.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Strong Wind Warning - Orange
entry.summary: Severe gale southeast winds gusting 130 km/h in about exposed valleys. Winds easing south of Hokitika mid to late Tuesday morning. Impact: Damage to trees, powerlines, and unsecured structures possible. Driving may be difficult, especially for high-sided vehicles and motorcycles. Chance of upgrading to a Red Warning: Low. Changes: End time extended.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Heavy Snow Watch
entry.summary: Periods of heavy snow above 700 metres, where snowfall amounts may approach warning criteria. Lesser amounts of snow down to 400 metres. Chance of upgrading to a Warning: Moderate. Changes: Chance of upgrade reduced to low.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Road Snowfall Warning
entry.summary: Further snow showers are possible during the night and 1 cm of snow may accumulate on the road above 800 metres.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Road Snowfall Warning
entry.summary: Snow is expected above 600 metres during the night and Tuesday morning. Accumulations of 10 to 15 cm are likely near the summit with lesser amounts down to 700 metres.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited
